prompt_text:
### Demographics: 52-year-old Asian woman

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 8 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes after ophthalmology referral for suspected retinopathy
Current symptoms: No DKA/HHS symptoms; no recent hypoglycemia episodes; no acute complaints; Despite repeated counseling on insulin need, the patient strongly refuses injections; shared decision was made to intensify oral therapy and reassess within 2-4 weeks.

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 3a (45-59)
Heart failure: HFmrEF (NYHA Class III)
ASCVD: Coronary artery disease (History of CABG); History of myocardial infarction
Body mass index category: Overweight
Diabetic retinopathy
Diabetic neuropathy
Diabetic Retinopathy
Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathy
Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
Major Depressive Disorder
Orthopedic fracture (healed)
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 3a (45-59); HF: HFmrEF (NYHA Class III); ASCVD: Coronary artery disease (History of CABG); MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Overweight
- Family history: No family history of type 2 diabetes (elicited)
- Social history: Never smoker; Alcohol 1-2 drinks/week

### Medication information
- Current medications: Metformin 1000mg twice daily; Empagliflozin 10mg once daily; Glimepiride 2mg once daily; Duloxetine (60 mg daily), Fluticasone/salmeterol Inhaler (twice daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Finerenone (10 mg daily)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 57.1 kg; Height 154.2 cm; BMI 24.0 kg/m²; Blood pressure 126/72 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 10.2%; 9 months ago 10.0%; 6 months ago 10.0%; 3 months ago 9.5%; current 10.1%), FPG (12 months ago 221mg/dL; 9 months ago 227mg/dL; 6 months ago 286mg/dL; 3 months ago 203mg/dL; current 300mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 58.9kg; 9 months ago 56.3kg; 6 months ago 56.8kg; 3 months ago 55.9kg; current 57.1kg), Height (12 months ago 154.2cm; 9 months ago 154.2cm; 6 months ago 154.2cm; 3 months ago 154.2cm; current 154.2cm), BMI (12 months ago 25.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 24.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 24.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 24.0kg/m²; current 24.0kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 126mmHg; 9 months ago 126mmHg; 6 months ago 125mmHg; 3 months ago 130mmHg; current 126mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 68mmHg; 9 months ago 69mmHg; 6 months ago 70mmHg; 3 months ago 71mmHg; current 72mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 30U/L; 9 months ago 33U/L; 6 months ago 23U/L; 3 months ago 26U/L; current 27U/L), AST (12 months ago 35U/L; 9 months ago 27U/L; 6 months ago 28U/L; 3 months ago 34U/L; current 29U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 1.3mg/dL; 9 months ago 1.3mg/dL; 6 months ago 1.2mg/dL; 3 months ago 1.2mg/dL; current 1.1mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 58mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 54mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 58mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 57mL/min/1.73m²; current 54mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 45mg/g; 9 months ago 65mg/g; 6 months ago 54mg/g; 3 months ago 60mg/g; current 75mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 77mg/dL; 9 months ago 82mg/dL; 6 months ago 83mg/dL; 3 months ago 77mg/dL; current 81mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 41mg/dL; 9 months ago 45mg/dL; 6 months ago 44mg/dL; 3 months ago 45mg/dL; current 43mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 165mg/dL; 9 months ago 150mg/dL; 6 months ago 165mg/dL; 3 months ago 164mg/dL; current 156mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 11.0g/dL; 9 months ago 11.0g/dL; 6 months ago 11.0g/dL; 3 months ago 11.8g/dL; current 11.1g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Negative (Current); fasting C-peptide Not measured (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited. Strong injection refusal is documented; under shared decision-making, the patient accepts oral regimen adjustment with short-interval reassessment.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
